message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): isolated unit test for race_with_cx_cancel helper (tick 408)

Added `race_with_cx_cancel_surfaces_cancel_on_pending_inner` — direct
unit test for the `race_with_cx_cancel` helper added by tick 387's
ft-l9mxa fix.

Isolation value: tick 380 + tick 389 exercise this helper end-to-end
through DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} against a stalled HTTP
server. That's the integration contract. But if the race mechanics
themselves regress (e.g. select! macro swap, cancel-watcher loop
bug, wrong error variant construction), the E2E tests would still
catch the symptom but with a harder-to-diagnose failure shape.

This isolated test exercises only the race itself:
1. Construct a cx.
2. Spawn a delayed cancel (50ms).
3. Feed `race_with_cx_cancel` a `std::future::pending::<Result<...>>()`
   — a future that will never complete on its own.
4. Assert return is Err(ClientError::Cancelled).
5. Assert elapsed < 1s (tight bound since no IO involved).

Observed elapsed: 105.8 ms — 50 ms cancel-fire delay + one 50 ms
cancel-watcher poll cycle + task scheduling overhead. Matches the
helper's design timing.

Complements tick 380 + 389 (E2E mid-flight cancel GET/POST) by
pinning the helper's core mechanics directly.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 32s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
